title: gonystylus eximius
intro: Gonystylus eximius, also known as the East Indian rosewood or Ramin, is a species of plant in the family Thymelaeaceae. It has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant effects. The health benefits of Gonystylus eximius include its ability to reduce fever, treat wounds, and alleviate respiratory issues. In herbal preparations, Gonystylus eximius can be found in various forms, such as essential oils, decoctions, and infusions, which can be used to make teas, tinctures, and topical creams.
